examples.description: primary-care visit — full note: brief chief complaint, structured history, past medical history and medications, vitals with units and a focused exam, and an assessment + plan synthesized and cited from the captured findings (Grounded Synthesis Mode, v1.1)

transcript.text: I've had a cough for about ten days now and it's not getting better.
It started as a dry cough but now I'm bringing up some yellow phlegm, and it's worse at night.
I had a fever the first few days, around thirty-eight and a half, but that's settled now. I still feel quite tired.
I tried over-the-counter cough syrup and some Tylenol but it hasn't helped much.
I have high blood pressure and I'm asthmatic — the asthma has been well controlled for years.
I take ramipril 5 milligrams once a day, and I use my salbutamol inhaler as needed.
Any allergies to medications? Yes, penicillin — it gives me a rash.
Let me take your vitals. Blood pressure is 132 over 84, heart rate is 88, temperature 37.1.
Oxygen saturation is 97 percent on room air, and your respiratory rate is 18.
On listening to your chest, there are coarse crackles at the right base. The left side is clear.
Your throat looks a little red but the tonsils are not enlarged, and there's no neck lymph node swelling.
I'd like to get a chest X-ray to look at that right base, and a sputum sample.
I'll start you on doxycycline 100 milligrams twice a day for seven days, since you're allergic to penicillin.
Keep using your salbutamol as needed, stay well hydrated, and rest.
Come back in one week, or sooner if you get short of breath or the fever returns.

claims.text: Cough for approximately ten days, not improving.
claims.source_quote: I've had a cough for about ten days now and it's not getting better.

claims.text: Cough began dry, now productive of yellow sputum and worse at night.
claims.source_quote: It started as a dry cough but now I'm bringing up some yellow phlegm, and it's worse at night.

claims.text: Fever to 38.5 in the first few days, now settled; ongoing fatigue.
claims.source_quote: I had a fever the first few days, around thirty-eight and a half, but that's settled now. I still feel quite tired.

claims.text: Tried over-the-counter cough syrup and acetaminophen with little relief.
claims.source_quote: I tried over-the-counter cough syrup and some Tylenol but it hasn't helped much.

claims.text: Hypertension; asthma, well controlled for years.
claims.source_quote: I have high blood pressure and I'm asthmatic — the asthma has been well controlled for years.

claims.text: Ramipril 5 mg orally once daily; salbutamol inhaler as needed.
claims.source_quote: I take ramipril 5 milligrams once a day, and I use my salbutamol inhaler as needed.
Yes, penicillin — it gives me a rash.

claims.text: Vitals: blood pressure 132/84 mmHg, heart rate 88 bpm, temperature 37.1.
claims.source_quote: Blood pressure is 132 over 84, heart rate is 88, temperature 37.1.

claims.text: Oxygen saturation 97% on room air; respiratory rate 18.
claims.source_quote: Oxygen saturation is 97 percent on room air, and your respiratory rate is 18.

claims.text: Chest auscultation: coarse crackles at the right base; left side clear.
claims.source_quote: On listening to your chest, there are coarse crackles at the right base. The left side is clear.

claims.text: Throat mildly erythematous, tonsils not enlarged; no cervical lymphadenopathy.
claims.source_quote: Your throat looks a little red but the tonsils are not enlarged, and there's no neck lymph node swelling.

claims.text: Working assessment: right basal lower respiratory tract infection — productive cough with prior fever, coarse crackles at the right base on auscultation.
claims.source_quote: On listening to your chest, there are coarse crackles at the right base. The left side is clear.

additional_sources.source_quote: It started as a dry cough but now I'm bringing up some yellow phlegm, and it's worse at night.
I had a fever the first few days, around thirty-eight and a half, but that's settled now. I still feel quite tired.

claims.text: No respiratory distress at presentation — oxygen saturation 97% on room air with a respiratory rate of 18.
claims.source_quote: Oxygen saturation is 97 percent on room air, and your respiratory rate is 18.

claims.text: Investigations: chest X-ray to assess the right base, and a sputum sample.
claims.source_quote: I'd like to get a chest X-ray to look at that right base, and a sputum sample.

claims.text: Treatment: doxycycline 100 mg twice daily for seven days, given penicillin allergy.
claims.source_quote: I'll start you on doxycycline 100 milligrams twice a day for seven days, since you're allergic to penicillin.

claims.text: Advice: continue salbutamol as needed, maintain hydration, and rest.
claims.source_quote: Keep using your salbutamol as needed, stay well hydrated, and rest.

claims.text: Follow-up: review in one week, or sooner if shortness of breath develops or fever returns.
claims.source_quote: Come back in one week, or sooner if you get short of breath or the fever returns.
